mechanism: Additive anticoagulant and antiplatelet effect
effect: Greatly increased risk of serious bleeding including internal hemorrhage
action: Avoid this combination. If both are necessary, use the lowest possible doses under strict medical supervision with regular INR monitoring.

mechanism: NSAIDs displace warfarin from protein binding and inhibit platelet aggregation
effect: Severely increased bleeding risk and gastrointestinal hemorrhage
action: Avoid NSAIDs with warfarin. Use Paracetamol for pain relief instead.

mechanism: NSAID displaces warfarin, increases anticoagulant effect
effect: Very high bleeding risk
action: Do not combine. Switch to Paracetamol for pain.

mechanism: NSAID-warfarin interaction causing displacement and platelet inhibition
effect: Critical bleeding risk
action: Avoid. Use Paracetamol instead.

mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits Warfarin metabolism via CYP1A2
effect: INR rises sharply, bleeding risk increases 2-3x
action: Monitor INR closely. Reduce Warfarin dose with antibiotic course.

mechanism: Amiodarone strongly inhibits CYP2C9 and CYP3A4, major enzymes for Warfarin metabolism
effect: INR can triple within 1-2 weeks, life-threatening bleeding
action: Reduce Warfarin dose by 30-50%. Monitor INR weekly for 6-8 weeks.

mechanism: Azithromycin may enhance warfarin effect via gut flora changes
effect: Increased INR, elevated bleeding risk
action: Monitor INR closely during and after antibiotic course.

mechanism: Both increase potassium retention. Combined effect causes severe hyperkalemia
effect: Dangerous potassium buildup (hyperkalemia) causing cardiac arrest
action: Monitor potassium levels weekly. Avoid in CKD patients.

mechanism: ACE inhibitor + potassium-sparing diuretic causes hyperkalemia
effect: Life-threatening high potassium causing cardiac arrhythmia
action: Regular potassium blood tests mandatory. Dose adjustment required.

mechanism: ARB + aldosterone antagonist synergistically increases serum potassium
effect: Severe hyperkalemia, cardiac arrest risk
action: Monitor potassium every 2 weeks. Avoid in kidney disease.

mechanism: Furosemide can impair kidney function, reducing Metformin clearance
effect: Increased risk of Metformin accumulation causing lactic acidosis
action: Monitor kidney function regularly. Reduce Metformin dose if eGFR drops.

mechanism: Furosemide causes potassium loss (hypokalemia), increasing Digoxin toxicity
effect: Digoxin toxicity — nausea, visual disturbances, dangerous arrhythmias
action: Monitor potassium and Digoxin levels. Consider potassium supplements.

mechanism: Thiazide causes hypokalemia, enhancing Digoxin toxicity at normal doses
effect: Digoxin toxicity with risk of fatal arrhythmia
action: Check Digoxin levels and serum potassium regularly.

mechanism: Amiodarone raises Digoxin levels by 70-100% via P-glycoprotein inhibition
effect: Digoxin toxicity — dangerous heart rhythm abnormalities
action: Reduce Digoxin dose by 50% when starting Amiodarone. Monitor levels weekly.

mechanism: Ibuprofen blocks Aspirin's access to COX-1, negating its cardioprotective platelet effect
effect: Aspirin's heart-protective effect is cancelled, increasing cardiac event risk
action: Take Aspirin at least 2 hours before Ibuprofen. Use Paracetamol as alternative.

mechanism: Dual antiplatelet therapy — additive bleeding effect
effect: Increased bleeding risk, GI hemorrhage
action: Usually intentional for cardiac patients. Add stomach protection (PPI). Monitor for unusual bleeding.

mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4, increasing Atorvastatin plasma levels
effect: Increased risk of myopathy and muscle breakdown (rhabdomyolysis)
action: Limit Atorvastatin to 20mg/day when on Amiodarone. Watch for muscle pain.

mechanism: Amiodarone severely inhibits Simvastatin metabolism, levels rise 5-10x
effect: High risk of rhabdomyolysis — severe muscle breakdown that can damage kidneys
action: Switch to Rosuvastatin or Pravastatin which are not metabolized by CYP3A4.

mechanism: Same drug class — duplicate statin therapy
effect: No added benefit, increased myopathy risk
action: Use only one statin. Consult cardiologist for optimal choice.

mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits CYP2D6 increasing Metoprolol levels, both slow heart rate
effect: Severe bradycardia (dangerously slow heart rate), heart block
action: Monitor heart rate. Reduce Metoprolol dose. Regular ECG.

mechanism: Additive AV node slowing effect
effect: Risk of symptomatic bradycardia and heart block
action: Monitor pulse daily. Avoid if resting heart rate is already below 60.

mechanism: Both prolong QT interval at the cardiac level
effect: QT prolongation leading to potentially fatal arrhythmia (Torsades de Pointes)
action: Avoid combining QT-prolonging drugs. ECG monitoring if combination is unavoidable.

mechanism: NSAIDs reduce prostaglandin-mediated kidney protection and reduce ACE inhibitor antihypertensive effect
effect: Acute kidney injury risk and loss of blood pressure control
action: Avoid NSAIDs in patients on ACE inhibitors. Use Paracetamol for pain.

mechanism: NSAIDs antagonize ARB kidney protection and raise blood pressure
effect: Kidney damage and hypertension worsening
action: Replace NSAID with Paracetamol. Monitor kidney function.

mechanism: Corticosteroids raise blood glucose, opposing Metformin's action
effect: Poor blood sugar control, hyperglycemia
action: Monitor blood glucose more frequently. Diabetes medications may need dose increase.

mechanism: Ciprofloxacin enhances insulin secretion stimulated by sulfonylureas
effect: Severe low blood sugar (hypoglycemia)
action: Monitor blood glucose closely during antibiotic course. Reduce sulfonylurea dose if needed.

mechanism: CNS depressant effect of benzodiazepine combined with beta blocker causing hypotension
effect: Dizziness, falls risk — especially dangerous in elderly
action: Use lowest effective Alprazolam dose. Avoid in elderly prone to falls.

mechanism: Two benzodiazepines — excessive CNS depression, respiratory suppression
effect: Oversedation, respiratory depression, fall and fracture risk
action: Never use two benzodiazepines together. Choose one and use at minimum dose.

mechanism: Losartan has mild uricosuric effect, potentially reducing need for Allopurinol
effect: Possible interaction, no major harm
action: Beneficial interaction for gout patients with hypertension. No action needed.

mechanism: ACE inhibitors increase risk of allopurinol hypersensitivity reactions
effect: Severe skin reactions (Stevens-Johnson syndrome) in rare cases
action: Monitor for skin rash, especially in first few weeks of therapy.

mechanism: Both compete for CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein; Colchicine levels can be elevated
effect: Colchicine toxicity — severe muscle and nerve damage
action: Use lowest effective Colchicine dose. Watch for muscle pain or weakness.

mechanism: Both steroids and NSAIDs damage stomach lining independently; combined effect is synergistic
effect: High risk of gastric ulcer and GI bleeding
action: Always add a PPI (Pantoprazole/Omeprazole) if both must be used. Avoid NSAID + steroid if possible.

mechanism: Additive GI irritation; corticosteroids reduce Aspirin absorption
effect: Increased GI bleeding risk
action: Use Pantoprazole as stomach protection if this combination is needed.

mechanism: Calcium binds to Levothyroxine in the gut, reducing its absorption
effect: Reduced thyroid hormone absorption, undertreated hypothyroidism
action: Take Levothyroxine on empty stomach. Space calcium dose by at least 4 hours.

mechanism: PPIs reduce stomach acid needed for Levothyroxine absorption
effect: Slightly reduced thyroid hormone absorption
action: Take Levothyroxine 30-60 minutes before breakfast, before any other medicine.

mechanism: Excessive BP drop (first-dose hypotension) when combining loop diuretic with ACE inhibitor
effect: Dizziness, falls, fainting — especially dangerous in elderly
action: Start ACE inhibitor at low dose. Monitor BP for 2 hours after first dose.

mechanism: Both prolong cardiac QT interval
effect: Life-threatening cardiac arrhythmia (Torsades de Pointes)
action: Avoid combination if possible. Choose alternative antibiotic. ECG monitoring mandatory.

mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits Warfarin metabolism
effect: Life-threatening bleeding. INR can triple.
action: Reduce Warfarin by 30-50%. Weekly INR checks for 2 months.
